title: Marbella Dolphin Watching Boat Hire: Costs, Tips & Best Boats 2026
html:
Marbella offers some of the Costa del Sol's finest dolphin watching, with regular sightings of bottlenose and striped dolphins just a few miles offshore. Departing from Puerto Banús or Marbella Marina, you can join a shared tour from around €45 per person or hire a private motorboat from €350 for a half-day. BoatHire24 lists a range of vessels to suit every budget.

Where to set off in Marbella

Puerto Banús is the most famous departure point, a glamorous marina packed with superyachts and charter companies. Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella) is a quieter alternative closer to the old town. For a shorter run to dolphin grounds, head from Cabopino Marina, near Artola dunes. The bay of Fuengirola, a short hop east, also has resident pods. Most skippers make for the Banco de la Plata or the waters off Cabo de Calaburras, where dolphins often feed. You can yacht charter in Marbella for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor yacht, Best for comfort and speed, with cabin space for 8, 12 guests; from €800 for a half-day.
  2. Catamaran, Ideal for stable family trips, carrying up to 12 passengers; from €1,200 for a full day.
  3. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), Nimble and fast, perfect for small groups of 5, 6; from €450 for three hours.
  4. Jet ski, Thrilling for a solo or couple dolphin chase, but limited to two people; from €150 per hour. See the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski charter page for a popular option.
  5. Luxury yacht (e.g. Sunseeker 63), Premium experience with crew, sun deck and galley; from €2,500 for eight hours.

How much does dolphin watching boat hire cost in Marbella?

Prices vary by boat type and duration, but shared tours start around €45 per person and private charters from €350.

  • Shared dolphin tour (2, 3 hours): €45, €60 per person.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours, up to 6 guests): €350, €600.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours, up to 12 guests): €1,200, €1,800.
  • Full-day crewed yacht charter in Marbella (accommodating up to 10): €2,000, €4,000.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella for dolphin watching?

Licence requirements depend on boat size and power; for most private charters under 15 metres, no licence is needed if a skipper is included. Spanish law requires a PNB (Patrón de Navegación Básica) for boats over 6 metres or 40 hp, but operators on BoatHire24 almost always provide a qualified skipper. Always confirm with the individual listing before booking. One option worth a look is the Sea-Doo GTX Jet Ski charter page.

Ask the skipper to head for the Banco de la Plata, a shallow bank about 5 nautical miles south of Marbella, where dolphins gather to feed in the morning. The best sightings are usually between May and September, when the Levante wind subsides and the sea is calm.

Season and weather for dolphin watching in Marbella

The prime months are June, July, August and September, with water temperatures above 20 °C and sea breezes light to moderate. The local Poniente wind, blowing from the west, often creates the flattest conditions for spotting pods. Trips run year-round but sightings are less reliable in the windier winter months.

At a glance

  • Price range: €45 shared tour to €4,000 crewed yacht
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 3, 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 12 on most boats
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a boat for dolphin watching without a skipper?

Bareboat rental is possible only if you hold a valid Spanish boating licence, but most dolphin-watching charters come with a skipper who knows the best spots.

How long does a dolphin watching trip from Marbella last?

Shared tours typically last two to three hours, while private charters can be booked for half-day (four hours) or full-day (eight hours) durations.

What type of dolphins are seen off Marbella?

The most common species are bottlenose dolphins and striped dolphins, with occasional sightings of pilot whales in deeper waters to the south.

Is there a best time of day for dolphin watching?

Morning trips (9 am, 12 pm) often yield the calmest seas and highest visibility, though late afternoon can also be productive.

BoatHire24 connects you with trusted local operators offering everything from jet skis to luxury motor yachts. If you own a boat in Marbella, you can become a host and earn income from your vessel. Book with confidence and enjoy the best dolphin encounters on the Costa del Sol.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
